mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate has been pinned near zero (0.12% as of Oct 2015) for years, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, the persistent low-rate regime continues to mechanically inflate present-value of future cash flows, producing a positive drift in S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If mean 20-day forward SPX return during periods with FEDFUNDS<0.25% is not significantly greater than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC<0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls to or below 5.0% (a level consistent with full employment), forward-looking markets price in an increased probability of monetary tightening within the next quarter, raising the discount rate applied to equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE<=5.0% triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC<0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS rising) combined with an accelerating federal funds rate path signals the Fed is validating labor market strength with tightening, creating a short-term risk-off reaction as markets reprice rate-hike timing; the joint occurrence of rising PAYEMS and rising FEDFUNDS produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days that neither series predicts alone.
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger sample shows no significant difference in 5-day forward SPX returns versus the unconditional distribution (p>0.10), or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over single-series models, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at elevated levels (near 1200k units) signal robust consumer demand and construction activity typical of mid-to-late expansion; in expansionary regimes this strength feeds through to broader corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns. In recessionary or contraction regimes the same signal reverses as overbuilding risk dominates.
failure_condition: If positive HOUST readings during expansion regimes fail to produce forward returns statistically distinguishable from zero over rolling 2-year windows, or the sign flips unexpectedly in non-recession periods, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline or stagnation in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening manufacturing supply-side momentum, which forward-looking equity markets price in as a leading indicator of slowing corporate earnings over the coming quarter, producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns whenever INDPRO trend turns negative.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=107.09 vs revised 100.75 as of 2015-09, indicating downward revision risk
failure_condition: If negative 3-month INDPRO trend periods do not show significantly lower forward 60-day SPX returns than positive-trend periods (difference-in-means p>0.05), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth above trend indicates robust consumer spending momentum; a monthly RSAFS increase exceeding 0.5% triggers upward revisions to near-term GDP and earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days until the growth impulse fades.
failure_condition: If triggered RSAFS surges are followed by positive 20-day forward SPX returns in less than 55% of cases, or Rank IC<0.03 over rolling 2-year windows, hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: As the Fed approaches its first rate hike after nearly a decade near zero (FEDFUNDS=0.12% in Oct 2015), markets in a liftoff-anticipation regime react to any confirmed upward move in FEDFUNDS with a short-term negative repricing of equities due to uncertainty over the pace of subsequent tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following a rate increase.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.12 as of 2015-10-01, market pricing December 2015 liftoff
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns following confirmed FEDFUNDS increases are positive in more than 55% of historical liftoff-analog instances, or effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of falling UNRATE and decelerating PAYEMS growth signals a labor market approaching capacity constraints with slowing job creation momentum, a combination that historically precedes late-cycle earnings deceleration and produces negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns beyond what either series predicts individually.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 5.0% AND PAYEMS 3-month average change decelerating relative to prior 6-month average
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightness with decelerating hiring
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger sample fails to show significantly lower 60-day forward SPX returns versus baseline (p>0.10), or the interaction effect does not outperform single-series UNRATE or PAYEMS models in out-of-sample Rank IC, hypothesis is rejected.
